title: Inspect /Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public for how Kernel and /vote are currently positioned. Focus on CODEX.md, README.md, .codex/prompts/kernel.md, .codex/prompts/vote.md, scripts/check-codex-kernel-prompt.sh, scripts/check-codex-vote-prompt.sh, scripts/local/launchers/*. Return the min...
summary_text: Outcome looks good and low-risk. Recommended minimal next actions (safety-first, low operator confusion): 1. **Preserve recovery points explicitly** - Push the archive commit: `git push -u origin codex/archive-xauto-prod-verify-gha-20260408`. - Optionally tag it: `git tag -a xauto-archive/9499be6c7 -m "Recovery snapshot before hardening merge" 9499be6c7 && git push origin xauto-archive/9499be6c7`. 2. **Keep PR branch as single source of truth** - Continue work only on `codex/kernel-orchestration-hardening-20260408`. - Avoid cherry-picking from archive branch unless recovery is required. 3. **Do not leave temporary/secondary workspace state lying around** - If you used a separate worktree for archival, delete it only after the archive is verified: `git worktree remove <path>`. - Verify no hidden references remain: `git worktree list`. 4. **Preserve rollback option** - Keep archive branch locally and remote until PR is merged and post-merge validation is complete. - If you need stronger rollback certainty, also store one-line recovery note with: `archive commit 9499be6c7`, source branch, and timestamp. 5. **Do the final “clean pass” once** - On PR branch and original worktree: `git status --short` should be clean. - On PR branch: optionally check last commit + CI artifact link once more after merge plan. 6. **Cleanup timing refinement** - Only delete `codex/archive-xauto-prod-verify-gha-20260408` after merge (or after a hold period if policy requires). This is the safest way to avoid accidental loss of a known-good recovery snapshot.
